reason: The statement applies because the text describes how the LORD, the God of Israel, was provoked to anger by the actions of Jeroboam, who made other gods and provoked the LORD by worshipping them. This provocation led to the LORD bringing evil upon Jeroboam's house and ultimately cutting off his descendants. This portrayal of the LORD's response to Jeroboam's actions can be interpreted as depicting Him as an angry God in this context.
